evals.name: publish-file-casual
evals.prompt: hey i've got an html file at /tmp/html-eval/landing.html — can you get it online and send me a public link?
evals.expected_output: The HTML file is published to ht-ml.app and the user is given a working public URL of the form https://<id>.ht-ml.app/ that serves the file's content, plus the update_key.
assertions: The reply contains a public URL matching https://<8-hex-id>.ht-ml.app/
An HTTP GET of that URL returns status 200
The served page contains the file's marker text 'Orbit Labs'
The reply communicates the update_key back to the user

evals.name: password-protected-precise
evals.prompt:
Publish this HTML as a private page that requires the password 'orca-sunset-42' to view:

Q3 board notes

Internal only.

. Only people with the password should be able to see it.
evals.expected_output: A live https://<id>.ht-ml.app/ URL that returns a 401 password prompt with no cookie and serves the content when the ht_ml_pwd cookie is set; the agent also notes the password is a shared secret to give to viewers.
assertions: The reply contains a public URL matching https://<8-hex-id>.ht-ml.app/
An HTTP GET of the URL with NO cookie returns status 401
An HTTP GET with cookie 'ht_ml_pwd=orca-sunset-42' returns status 200 and shows 'Q3 board notes'
The reply tells the user the password is a shared secret that must be given to anyone who should access the page

evals.name: no-html-use-template
evals.prompt: I want to put up a little webpage with this week's status update for my team, but I don't have any HTML written. Can you whip one up and host it?
evals.expected_output: The agent uses one of the skill's templates (ideally status-report.html), fills it with status content, publishes it, and returns a working https://<id>.ht-ml.app/ URL.
assertions: The agent uses or adapts a template from assets/templates/ (ideally status-report.html) rather than writing from scratch
The reply contains a public URL matching https://<8-hex-id>.ht-ml.app/
An HTTP GET of that URL returns status 200
The served page reads like a status report (contains status sections such as Shipped / In progress / Highlights)
